unassigned_stints.reason: The substantive match is excellent (colony Mauritius, 'asst. audr.' vs 'Assistant Auditor', start 1936, initials J.L.), but the dossier states this stint is also gate-compatible with bio 'tadman_john-ladbrooke_e1931' outside this case, 'already linked elsewhere or filtered', and Phase 1 dropped this pairing as ambiguous for exactly that competitor. 'ADMAN, JOHN LAD BROOKE' is almost certainly an OCR mutilation of 'TADMAN, JOHN LADBROOKE' — i.e. the competitor bio is the same person under its correct spelling. Assigning the stint to this mangled duplicate record would risk double-assignment against the canonical Tadman record; the link should be resolved by merging/deduplicating the two bios, not by an assignment here.

Different person: 'Expert Adviser, Mines Department' 1911-1919 (C.M.G. 1918, K.C.M.G. 1919) is the mining/petroleum adviser John Cadman (gate-compatible with 'cadman_john_b1877'). The bio's career begins with a 1929 Oxford exhibition and a 1931 entry into the colonial audit department; he cannot have been an expert adviser in 1911. No place, position, date, honour, or edition agreement (all deterministic checks beyond name FAIL).

Same Mines Department Expert Adviser ('Prof. Sir J. Cadman', K.C.M.G.) as the Trinidad 1911 stint, listed 1909-1921. Chronologically impossible for the bio (career starts 1929/1931, audit branch, Uganda/Mauritius); no dimension agrees beyond the fuzzy surname.

Same mines Inspector/Expert Adviser as the other two Cadman stints, listed 1906-1922 with K.C.M.G. by 1922. The bio's subject was a schoolboy/undergraduate during most of this span and served in the audit department in East Africa and Mauritius, never in mines or Trinidad/Tobago; every deterministic check beyond surname FAILs.

notes: Bio 'adman_john-lad-brooke_e1929' (printed 'ADMAN, JOHN LAD BROOKE') is almost certainly an OCR-corrupted duplicate of the out-of-case biography 'tadman_john-ladbrooke_e1931' ('TADMAN, JOHN LADBROOKE'): identical forenames modulo a split ('LAD BROOKE' = 'LADBROOKE'), initial 'T' dropped from the surname, and a career line ('asst. audr., Mauritius, Feb., 1936') matching the Tadman/Mauritius staff-list stint on place, position, and date. Recommend merging the two bio records; once merged, the 'Tadman, J. L___Mauritius___1936' stint assignment is effectively certain. The three Cadman stints belong to Sir John Cadman (b. 1877), the Mines Department expert adviser, and should resolve to 'cadman_john_b1877'. duplicate_bio_groups left empty only because the duplicate bio is outside this case.
